question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, showing a 0.3% monthly increase in the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers. Since 0.3% is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for YES resolution, the market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty. The current market price of 65% appears to reflect pre-release uncertainty that no longer exists.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased only 0.3%
0.3% is far below the 0.8% threshold needed for YES resolution
Market resolution is now deterministic based on published data
Report was released April 10, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET as scheduled
risks: Potential data revision by BLS (though unlikely for such large changes)
Misinterpretation of resolution criteria (though criteria appear clear)
Market may not have fully processed the released data yet
